title: Blephariglottis longicornu

The plant is native to the tropical forests of Asia. Blephariglottis longicornu is a terrestrial orchid, growing on the ground in shaded areas. The exact classification of Blephariglottis longicornu may be subject to change as new research emerges.

intro: Blephariglottis longicornu, also known as Salvia longicornu, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family. This plant is known for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a popular natural remedy for wound healing, and its ability to support respiratory health by helping to relieve congestion and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Blephariglottis longicornu include diterpenoids and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Blephariglottis longicornu is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves for its wound-healing and anti-inflammatory effects.
